title: AtlasIQ Ops
summary: I built AtlasIQ Ops as an AI-powered visual knowledge transformation platform that turns raw learning material into summaries, concept maps, charts, cheat notes, flashcards, and review workflows.
proof: This project proves full-stack product execution across authentication, per-user workspaces, secure upload workflows, AI-generated learning assets, visual dashboards, and Render deployment.
details: Built authenticated per-user workspaces with register/login/session flows and server-backed storage.
Created a multi-output insight studio for summaries, concept graphs, charts, cheat notes, flashcards, citations, timelines, and recommendations.
Polished the interface with premium light-study visuals, responsive grids, animation layers, and recruiter-friendly demo access.
architecture: Express serves the product UI, auth APIs, workspace APIs, exports, and AI generation routes from one Render URL.
Session tokens and password hashes protect user access while workspace records stay user-scoped in the server data store.
Content ingestion accepts pasted text, source links, file metadata, role, audience, and difficulty settings.
The generation layer returns structured knowledge assets that feed cards, graphs, review mode, analytics, and exports.
Render auto-deploys the GitHub-backed service with persistent disk-backed data configuration.
tradeoff: I prioritized a deployable single-service product with polished workflow depth first; deeper document parsing, OCR, and team collaboration can be expanded as the next enterprise layer.
